evals.prompt: Write release notes for the commit history in commits.txt.
evals.expected_output: A RELEASE_NOTES.md file grouping the changes into Breaking Changes, Features, Fixes and Performance sections, with the TOML config migration highlighted as breaking, written in user-facing language, containing only changes that appear in commits.txt.
assertions: The release notes group changes into sections such as breaking changes, features, and fixes
The breaking change about switching the config file format from INI to TOML is mentioned prominently and includes migration guidance
The release notes do not mention any feature, fix, or change that is not present in commits.txt
The release notes are written in user-facing language rather than raw commit messages

evals.prompt: We are about to cut a release. Turn the commit history in commits.txt into polished release notes for our users.
evals.expected_output: A RELEASE_NOTES.md file grouping the changes into sections, with the breaking TOML config change highlighted, containing only changes that appear in commits.txt.
assertions: The release notes group changes into sections such as breaking changes, features, and fixes
The breaking change about switching the config file format from INI to TOML is mentioned prominently
The release notes do not mention any feature, fix, or change that is not present in commits.txt

evals.prompt: How many commits are listed in commits.txt? Just give me the number.
evals.expected_output: The skill should NOT trigger. The agent only answers the counting question (10 commits) without creating release notes.
assertions: A new git branch was created
The agent did NOT create a RELEASE_NOTES.md file or any other new file, and only answered the counting question
